deck_title: Trait Tracker: How Living Things Pass On Traits
subject: Science
differentiation: Two reading levels; sentence frames for emerging readers; an extension question for early finishers.

slides.title: Trait Tracker
bullets: How living things pass on their traits
slides.notes: Suggested time: 1 min. Welcome routine while the class settles.

slides.title: Do Now
bullets: Look at the puppy and its parents on the board.
What do you notice that looks the same?
slides.notes: Suggested time: 4 min. Opening check-for-understanding routine. Emerging readers: offer the sentence frame 'The puppy and its parent both have ___.'
slides.check_for_understanding: Can students name at least one shared trait between parent and offspring?

slides.title: What Is a Trait?
bullets: A trait is something you can observe about a living thing.
Some traits change with the environment.
slides.notes: Suggested time: 6 min. Anchor the vocabulary word 'trait'. On-level readers define in their own words; emerging readers match trait cards to pictures.

slides.title: Activity: Track the Trait
bullets: 2. List two traits you can observe.
3. Decide: inherited or from the environment?
slides.notes: Suggested time: 12 min. The step-by-step routine pulled from the lesson plan. Extension for early finishers: find a trait that could be both inherited and shaped by the environment.

slides.title: Wrap Up
bullets: Some traits are shaped by where a living thing lives.
slides.notes: Suggested time: 3 min. Exit ticket: each student writes one inherited trait and one environmental trait.
